1600	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"binary")?;
1601	        // C: cl = luaU_undump(L, p->z, p->name)
1602	        // TODO(port): undump returns a LClosure; the Rust API isn't finalised.
1603	        crate::undump::undump(state, &mut p.z, &p.name)?
1604	    } else {
1605	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "text")
1606	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"text")?;
1607	        // C: cl = luaY_parser(L, p->z, &p->buff, &p->dyd, p->name, c)
1608	        // TODO(port): parser API not yet finalised; returns a LClosure.
1609	        parse_stub(state, &mut p.z, &mut p.buff, &mut p.dyd, &p.name, c)?
1610	    };
1611	
1612	    // C: lua_assert(cl->nupvalues == cl->p->sizeupvalues)
1613	    debug_assert!(cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len());
1614	    // C: luaF_initupvals(L, cl)
1615	    func::init_upvals(state, &cl)?;
1616	
1617	    // PORT NOTE: In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` / `luaU_undump` themselves push the
1618	    // closure onto the stack before returning (see lparser.c `luaY_parser`:
1619	    // `setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L);`). In the Rust port
1620	    // they return the closure by value, so `f_parser` must push it here.
1621	    // Without this, the caller (`api::load`) sees stale Nil at top-1 and any
1622	    // subsequent `pcall_k(state, 0, ...)` fails with "attempt to call a nil
1623	    // value".
1624	    state.check_stack(1)?;
1625	    state.push(LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)));
1626	
1627	    Ok(())
1628	}
1629	
1630	/// Loads and parses a chunk in protected mode, returning the status.
1631	///
1632	/// C: `int luaD_protectedparser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1633	pub(crate) fn protected_parser(
1634	    state: &mut LuaState,
1635	    z: ZIO,
1636	    name: &[u8],
1637	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1638	) -> LuaStatus {
1639	    // C: incnny(L)  — cannot yield during parsing
1640	    // incnny → state.inc_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1641	    state.inc_nny();
1642	
1643	    let mut p = SParser {
1644	        z,
1645	        buff: LexBuffer::new(),
1646	        dyd: DynDataStub::new(),
1647	        mode: mode.map(|m| m.to_vec()),
1648	        name: name.to_vec(),
1649	    };
1650	
1651	    // C: luaZ_initbuffer(L, &p.buff) — LexBuffer::new() already initialised above
1652	    // (macros.tsv: luaZ_initbuffer → buf.init() / Mbuffer::new())
1653	
1654	    // C: status = luaD_pcall(L, f_parser, &p, savestack(L, L->top.p), L->errfunc)
1655	    let top_idx = state.top_idx();
1656	    let errfunc = state.errfunc;
1657	    let status = pcall(state, |s| f_parser(s, &mut p), top_idx, errfunc);
1658	
1659	    // C: luaZ_freebuffer(L, &p.buff) — Rust's Drop handles deallocation (macros.tsv)
1660	    // C: luaM_freearray(L, p.dyd.actvar.arr, ...) — Rust's Drop handles Vec (macros.tsv)
1661	    // (p and all its sub-fields drop here automatically)
1662	
1663	    // C: decnny(L)
1664	    // decnny → state.dec_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1665	    state.dec_nny();
1666	
1667	    status
1668	}
1669	
1670	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1671	// PORT STATUS
1672	//   source:        src/ldo.c  (1029 lines, ~37 functions translated, 2 omitted)
1673	//   target_crate:  lua-vm
1674	//   confidence:    medium
1675	//   todos:         23
1676	//   port_notes:    13
1677	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
1678	//   notes:         Core call/stack/error machinery translated faithfully.
1679	//                  setjmp/longjmp → Result<T,LuaError> throughout.
1680	//                  relstack/correctstack omitted (StackIdx already offset-based).
1681	//                  Coroutine functions (lua_resume, lua_yieldk, resume, unroll,
1682	//                  etc.) are translated but require Phase E stack-switching to
1683	//                  actually work.  Hook-callback borrow conflict flagged as
1684	//                  TODO(port) in hook() and finish_ccall(); Phase E must solve.
1685	//                  All method calls (check_stack, gc_check_step, get_ci*,
1686	//                  set_ci*, next_ci, etc.) are best-guess stubs to be wired
1687	//                  up in Phase B once the LuaState API is finalised.
1688	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1689

1540	// Protected parser
1541	// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1542	
1543	/// Parser invocation data passed through `pcall`.
1544	///
1545	/// C: `struct SParser { ZIO *z; Mbuffer buff; Dyndata dyd; const char *mode; const char *name; }`
1546	///
1547	/// PORT NOTE: `const char *mode` and `const char *name` become owned byte vecs
1548	/// so that `SParser` can outlive the original string data without raw pointers.
1549	struct SParser {
1550	    z: ZIO,
1551	    /// LexBuffer from `crate::zio` (Mbuffer in C).
1552	    buff: LexBuffer,
1553	    /// TODO(phase-b): real Dyndata lives in the lua-parse crate.
1554	    dyd: DynDataStub,
1555	    // C: const char *mode — byte slice for chunk mode ("b", "t", or "bt")
1556	    // PORT NOTE: stored as Option<Vec<u8>> to own the bytes; None means no mode restriction.
1557	    mode: Option<Vec<u8>>,
1558	    // C: const char *name — chunk name (source identifier)
1559	    name: Vec<u8>,
1560	}
1561	
1562	/// Checks that the chunk mode permits loading the given kind ("binary" or "text").
1563	///
1564	/// C: `static void checkmode(lua_State *L, const char *mode, const char *x)`
1565	fn check_mode(
1566	    state: &mut LuaState,
1567	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1568	    kind: &[u8],
1569	) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1570	    if let Some(mode_bytes) = mode {
1571	        // C: strchr(mode, x[0]) == NULL  — mode doesn't contain the first letter of kind
1572	        let kind_char = kind[0];
1573	        if !mode_bytes.contains(&kind_char) {
1574	            // C: luaO_pushfstring + luaD_throw(L, LUA_ERRSYNTAX)
1575	            // TODO(port): &[u8] display — lossy UTF-8 here is acceptable for mode/kind
1576	            // strings which are always ASCII literals ("binary"/"text" and "bt"/"b"/"t").
1577	            return Err(LuaError::syntax(format_args!(
1578	                "attempt to load a {} chunk (mode is '{}')",
1579	                core::str::from_utf8(kind).unwrap_or("?"),
1580	                core::str::from_utf8(mode_bytes).unwrap_or("?"),
1581	            )));
1582	        }
1583	    }
1584	    Ok(())
1585	}
1586	
1587	/// Parser callback invoked inside `pcall`: reads the first byte to decide
1588	/// binary vs. text, then calls the undumper or parser accordingly.
1589	///
1590	/// C: `static void f_parser(lua_State *L, void *ud)`
1591	fn f_parser(state: &mut LuaState, p: &mut SParser) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1592	    // C: int c = zgetc(p->z)  — read first character
1593	    // zgetc → z.getc()  (macros.tsv)
1594	    let c = p.z.getc();
1595	
1596	    // C: if (c == LUA_SIGNATURE[0])
1597	    // LUA_SIGNATURE → const LUA_SIGNATURE: &[u8] = b"\x1bLua"  (macros.tsv)
1598	    let cl = if c == b'\x1b' as i32 {
1599	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "binary")
1600	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"binary")?;
1601	        // C: cl = luaU_undump(L, p->z, p->name)
1602	        // TODO(port): undump returns a LClosure; the Rust API isn't finalised.
1603	        crate::undump::undump(state, &mut p.z, &p.name)?
1604	    } else {

delta.text: Fix verified — the original
`Syntax: syntax error (load_string status=1)` is gone, replaced with a different
error (a debug_assert panic on `maxstacksize >= freereg`) which is the next iteration's bl

ocker.

## Report

**Root cause:** `lua_types::LuaString::is_reserved_word()` at `crates/lua-types
/src/string.rs:29-32` always returned `false` (it was a `TODO(port)` stub). The lex
er's identifier scanner at `crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs:1875` consulted this method to dist
inguish keywords from names, so every Lua keyword — `local`, `if`, `while`, `function`, `return`, etc. — was em
itted as `TK_NAME` (292). The parser then routed `local x = 5; print(x)` through `exprstat` instead of `localst
at`, parsed `local` as a global variable reference, hit `print` as the next N
AME at the spot where a `=` or `(` was expected, and bailed out of `exprstat` with `

"syntax error"` because `v.k` was `IndexUp` rather than `Call`.

**The
fix** (`crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs:1869-1893`): bypass the bro
ken `extra`/`is_reserved_word` path entirely. After interning the identifier bytes, look the
slice up in `LUAX_TOKENS[..NUM_RESERVED]` — those entries are the
22 keywords in declaration order, so the matched index plus `FIRST_RESERVED` yields the correct `TK_*
` token id (e.g. `local` → index 12 → 269 = `TK_LOCAL`). On miss,
return `TK_NAME` as before. This sidesteps the missing `extra` accessor on the canonical `LuaString` and
resolves the `todo!()` that was poisoning the keyword arm. All temporary instrumentation was removed.
**New error after fix:** `[panic] assertion failed: ls.fs.as_ref().unwrap().f.maxstacksize >= ls.fs.as_ref().unw
rap().freereg` — a different blocker (codegen not bumping `maxstacksize` to
keep up with `freereg`), for the next loop iteration.

content.text:
Fix verified — the original `Syntax: syntax error (load_string status=1)` is gone, replaced with a different error (a debug_assert panic on `maxstacksize >= freereg`) which is the next iteration's blocker.

## Report

**Root cause:** `lua_types::LuaString::is_reserved_word()` at `crates/lua-types/src/string.rs:29-32` always returned `false` (it was a `TODO(port)` stub). The lexer's identifier scanner at `crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs:1875` consulted this method to distinguish keywords from names, so every Lua keyword — `local`, `if`, `while`, `function`, `return`, etc. — was emitted as `TK_NAME` (292). The parser then routed `local x = 5; print(x)` through `exprstat` instead of `localstat`, parsed `local` as a global variable reference, hit `print` as the next NAME at the spot where a `=` or `(` was expected, and bailed out of `exprstat` with `"syntax error"` because `v.k` was `IndexUp` rather than `Call`.

**The fix** (`crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs:1869-1893`): bypass the broken `extra`/`is_reserved_word` path entirely. After interning the identifier bytes, look the slice up in `LUAX_TOKENS[..NUM_RESERVED]` — those entries are the 22 keywords in declaration order, so the matched index plus `FIRST_RESERVED` yields the correct `TK_*` token id (e.g. `local` → index 12 → 269 = `TK_LOCAL`). On miss, return `TK_NAME` as before. This sidesteps the missing `extra` accessor on the canonical `LuaString` and resolves the `todo!()` that was poisoning the keyword arm. All temporary instrumentation was removed.

**New error after fix:** `[panic] assertion failed: ls.fs.as_ref().unwrap().f.maxstacksize >= ls.fs.as_ref().unwrap().freereg` — a different blocker (codegen not bumping `maxstacksize` to keep up with `freereg`), for the next loop iteration.
